title: Harness Doctrine Enforcement
description: Machine-readable rules for agent execution discipline in EndeavorEverlasting/BlacksmithGuild

enforcementRules.description: Every substantial response must open with [TBG | Sprint/PR | lane/context | branch: <branch>] and name repo, branch, PR, lane, owned scope, forbidden scope, expected artifacts, and validation order
Every serious task must follow: request -> evidence review -> bounded decision -> mutation -> artifacts -> validation -> report -> next decision
Inspect before inventing. Read before writing. Grep before assuming.
Reuse .tbg/workflows, scripts/tbg, validators, and helpers before creating new ones
Checkpoint coherent progress. Do not reset, force-push, or delete to make the floor clean.
A file must change, a branch must exist, a PR must be open, or a test must pass. Words alone are not completion.
If the user specifies a validation order, lane, or forbidden scope, those override the defaults
Any prompt claiming to install, set up, build, execute, repair, configure, upgrade, deploy, merge, or release must produce the corresponding mutation and proof
contract -> harness -> static test -> build -> launcher -> command ACK -> behavior observed -> live runtime. Do not claim a higher level from a lower one.
Every serious session must end with completed work, files changed, artifacts, validation, gaps, risks, Git/PR state, and one exact next command

Every script that sends keyboard/mouse input to the Bannerlord game must call Assert-TbgGameUnpaused.ps1 before sending any gameplay keys. Blind ESC is forbidden — read the runtime regent first to detect actual pause/menu state.
